# Heddle

> Agent-native version control. Heddle is built around saved states and isolated threads with signed provenance, and exposes a stable JSON machine contract (`--output json`) so agents can drive it without parsing prose. Git compatibility is an interop/output layer, not the core model.

This index covers Heddle's top-level reference docs. Start an agent at `heddle help model` for the mental model and `heddle help --output json` for the machine-readable command catalog; the docs below carry the durable contracts (exit codes, JSON schemas, stability guarantees, auth model).

## Docs

- [Self-sovereign auth: minting and attenuating your own Biscuit](SELF_SOVEREIGN_AUTH.md): A self-sovereign Heddle client is one that holds its own root

- [Heddle 1.0 stability criterion](STABILITY.md): Status: strawman. Every <TBD: maintainer> marker is a policy
- [Verification And Git Projection Cleanup Plan](VERIFICATION_CLEANUP_PLAN.md): Status: cleanup plan from the July 2026 branch review and follow-up design interview.

- [Phase 4 (Wire Protocol) Summary](WIRE_PROTOCOL.md): This phase adds a minimal, local-testable wire protocol and remote operations (push/pull) for Heddle.
- [Workspace Split Plan](WORKSPACE_SPLIT_PLAN.md): Status: Phase 1 executed on 2026-05-13. The workspace now lives across three repos:
- [Heddle CLI exit codes](exit-codes.md): The CLI follows BSD sysexits.h so codes mean the same thing to humans, init
- [.heddleignore](heddleignore.md): .heddleignore is heddle's per-repo file for telling heddle capture,

- [heddle undo](undo.md): A safety net for the operations a daily user is most likely to want to roll back.
- [Unwrap and Expect Policy](unwrap-policy.md): Heddle should reach zero production unwrap and expect call sites before 1.0. Until that cleanup is complete, the gate is per-crate: crates that already have...

## Reference directories

Not inlined into `llms-full.txt`; browse in-repo under `docs/`.

- [adr/](adr/): Architecture Decision Records.
- [agents/](agents/): Per-repo agent operating config (issue tracker, domain docs).
- [design/](design/): Design notes and proposals.
- [contributor-guide/](contributor-guide/): Contributor onboarding and workflow guides.
- [perf/](perf/): Performance investigations and profiles.
- [benchmarks/](benchmarks/): Benchmark harness notes and results.
- [spikes/](spikes/): Exploratory spikes and prototypes.
- [program/](program/): Program-level planning docs.
